concept: Formulate a racing game where vehicles are powered by momentum conservation rather than conventional engines, requiring strategic use of gravity, collisions, and environmental interactions to maintain speed. Courses feature momentum transfer stations where excess kinetic energy can be temporarily banked for later withdrawal, creating strategic decisions about when to sacrifice current speed for future acceleration. Vehicle customization focuses on mass distribution, friction coefficients, and elastic properties rather than traditional engine upgrades. Advanced racing techniques involve creating resonant harmonic movements that gradually amplify momentum without external energy input. The championship takes place on a course that periodically inverts its gravitational field, requiring mastery of momentum conservation through gravitational transition points.
